prerequisites: An Auth0 tenant. The tenant domain becomes the connector's base_url config (for example https://your-tenant.us.auth0.com).
Authorization to call the Auth0 Management API v2, which is the API this source reads from (users, clients, connections, roles, organizations).

Either a Management API access token, or a Machine-to-Machine (M2M) application's Client ID and Client Secret authorized for the Management API. These supply the credentials config object the connector requires.

authMethods.name: Management API access token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: Supply a Management API v2 access token as credentials.access_token. The connector sends it directly as a Bearer token on every request. The Management API audience is https://your-tenant/api/v2/ and Auth0 issues these tokens with a default lifetime of 24 hours, so a static token is best for short-lived or test use.

authMethods.name: OAuth2 client credentials (Machine-to-Machine)
authMethods.summary: Supply credentials.client_id and credentials.client_secret from an Auth0 Machine-to-Machine application authorized for the Management API. The connector runs the client credentials grant against your tenant's /oauth/token endpoint with grant_type=client_credentials and an audience (defaulting to https://your-tenant/api/v2/) to obtain an access token automatically. This is the recommended mode for unattended or ongoing syncs.

setupSteps.title: Set your tenant domain as base_url
setupSteps.body: Set the required base_url config to your Auth0 tenant domain, for example https://your-tenant.us.auth0.com. The connector validates that it is an absolute https URL with a host and uses it to reach the Management API v2 (api/v2/...) and, in M2M mode, the /oauth/token endpoint.

setupSteps.title: Choose an authentication mode
setupSteps.body: Decide between a static Management API access token (credentials.access_token) or Machine-to-Machine client credentials (credentials.client_id + credentials.client_secret). Provide whichever set you choose inside the required credentials config object. If an access_token is present the connector uses it as a Bearer token; otherwise it falls back to the client credentials grant.

setupSteps.title: For M2M: create and authorize a Machine-to-Machine application
setupSteps.body: In the Auth0 Dashboard create a Machine-to-Machine application and authorize it for the Auth0 Management API, granting the Management API permissions/scopes needed for the streams you sync (for example read:users, read:clients, read:connections, read:roles, read:organizations). Copy the application's Client ID and Client Secret into credentials.client_id and credentials.client_secret.

setupSteps.title: Optionally set a start date
setupSteps.body: Set the optional start_date config to bound incremental reads. Auth0 streams begin with an empty incremental cursor (full sync), and start_date raises that floor at read time.

setupSteps.title: Verify the connection
setupSteps.body: Run the connector's check, which performs a bounded read of the Management API clients list (api/v2/clients with per_page=1) to confirm the base_url, credentials, and required Management API permissions are valid before syncing.

sources.title: Self-Service Machine-to-Machine for the Management API
